member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.Assistant.Object
member.summary: The object type, which is always `assistant`.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.Assistant.NucleusSamplingFactor
member.summary:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.
We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantClient.GetMessagesAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.ClientModel.Primitives.RequestOptions)
member.summary: [Protocol Method] Returns a paginated collection of messages for a given thread.
member.param: The ID of the [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads) the messages belong to.
A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 20.
Sort order by the `created_at` timestamp of the objects. `asc` for ascending order and`desc` for descending order. Allowed values: "asc" | "desc"

A cursor for use in pagination. `after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with obj_foo, your subsequent call can include after=obj_foo in order to fetch the next page of the list.

A cursor for use in pagination. `before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with obj_foo, your subsequent call can include before=obj_foo in order to fetch the previous page of the list.
The request options, which can override default behaviors of the client pipeline on a per-call basis.
member.exception: is an empty string, and was expected to be non-empty.
Service returned a non-success status code.
member.returns: A collection of service responses, each holding a page of values.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantCreationOptions
member.summary: Represents additional options available when creating a new .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantCreationOptions.Tools
member.summary:
A list of tool enabled on the assistant.
There can be a maximum of 128 tools per assistant. Tools can be of types `code_interpreter`, `file_search`, or `function`.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantCreationOptions.NucleusSamplingFactor
member.summary:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.
We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantDeletionResult.Object
member.summary: The object type, which is always `assistant.deleted`.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantModificationOptions
member.summary: Represents additional options available when modifying an existing .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantModificationOptions.Model
member.summary: The replacement model that the assistant should use.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantModificationOptions.DefaultTools
member.summary:
A list of tool enabled on the assistant.
There can be a maximum of 128 tools per assistant. Tools can be of types `code_interpreter`, `file_search`, or `function`.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantModificationOptions.NucleusSamplingFactor
member.summary:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.
We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantThread.Object
member.summary: The object type, which is always `thread`.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.AssistantThread.ToolResources
member.summary: The set of resources that are made available to the assistant's tools on this thread. The resources are specific to the type of tool. For example, the `code_interpreter` tool requires a list of file IDs, while the `file_search` tool requires a list of vector store IDs.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Assistants.MessageImageDetail.Auto
member.summary: Default. Allows the model to automatically select detail.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Assistants.MessageImageDetail.Low
member.summary: Reduced detail that uses fewer tokens than .

member.name: F:OpenAI.Assistants.MessageImageDetail.High
member.summary: Increased detail that uses more tokens than .

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions
member.summary: Represents additional options available when creating a new .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.ModelOverride
member.summary: A run-specific model name that will override the assistant's defined model. If not provided, the assistant's selection will be used.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.InstructionsOverride
member.summary: A run specific replacement for the assistant's default instructions that will override the assistant-level instructions. If not specified, the assistant's instructions will be used.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.AdditionalInstructions
member.summary: Run-specific additional instructions that will be appended to the assistant-level instructions solely for this run. Unlike , the assistant's instructions are preserved and these additional instructions are concatenated.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.AdditionalMessages
member.summary: Adds additional messages to the thread before creating the run.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.AllowParallelToolCalls
member.summary: Whether to enable parallel function calling during tool use.
member.remarks: Assumed true if not otherwise specified.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.ToolsOverride
member.summary:
A run-specific collection of tool definitions that will override the assistant-level defaults. If not provided, the assistant's defined tools will be used. Available tools include:
code_interpreter - - works with data, math, and computer code
file_search - - dynamically enriches an Run's context with content from vector stores
function - - enables caller-provided custom functions for actions and enrichment

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.Metadata
member.summary: Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Keys can be a maximum of 64 characters long and values can be a maximum of 512 characters long.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.Temperature
member.summary: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.NucleusSamplingFactor
member.summary:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.
We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.MaxInputTokenCount
member.summary: The maximum number of prompt tokens that may be used over the course of the run. The run will make a best effort to use only the number of prompt tokens specified, across multiple turns of the run. If the run exceeds the number of prompt tokens specified, the run will end with status `incomplete`. See `incomplete_details` for more info.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.MaxOutputTokenCount
member.summary: The maximum number of completion tokens that may be used over the course of the run. The run will make a best effort to use only the number of completion tokens specified, across multiple turns of the run. If the run exceeds the number of completion tokens specified, the run will end with status `incomplete`. See `incomplete_details` for more info.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunCreationOptions.TruncationStrategy
member.summary: Gets or sets the truncation strategy.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.RunModificationOptions
member.summary: Represents additional options available when modifying an existing .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunStatus.IsTerminal
member.summary: [Helper property] Gets a value indicating whether this run status represents a condition wherein the run can no longer continue.
member.remarks: For more information, please refer to: https://platform.openai.com/docs/assistants/how-it-works/run-lifecycle

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.RunTruncationStrategy
member.summary: Controls for how a thread will be truncated prior to the run. Use this to control the initial context window of the run.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunTruncationStrategy.LastMessages
member.summary: The number of most recent messages from the thread when constructing the context for the run.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.RunTruncationStrategy.Auto
member.summary: The default that will eliminate messages in the middle of the thread to fit within the context length of the model or the max prompt tokens.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Assistants.RunTruncationStrategy.CreateLastMessagesStrategy(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new instance using the last_messages strategy type, which will truncate the thread to specified count of preceding messages for the run.
member.param: The count of last messages that the run should evaluate.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.MessageContentUpdate
member.summary: Represents a streaming update to content as part of the Assistants API.
member.remarks: Distinct instances will be generated for each part and each content subcomponent, such as instances, even if this information arrived in the same response chunk.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.MessageContentUpdate.TextAnnotation
member.summary: An update to an annotation associated with a specific content item in the message's content items collection.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.MessageStatusUpdate
member.summary: The update type presented when the status of a message changes.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.RequiredActionUpdate
member.summary: The update type presented when the status of a has changed to requires_action, indicating that tool output submission or another intervention is needed for the run to continue.
member.remarks: Distinct instances will generated for each required action, meaning that parallel function calling will present multiple updates even if the tool calls arrive at the same time.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Assistants.RequiredActionUpdate.GetThreadRun

member.name: T:OpenAI.Assistants.StreamingUpdate
member.summary: Represents a single item of streamed Assistants API data.
member.remarks:
Please note that this is the abstract base type. To access data, downcast an instance of this type to an appropriate, derived update type:
For messages: ,
For runs and run steps: , , ,
For threads:

member.name: P:OpenAI.Assistants.StreamingUpdate.UpdateKind
member.summary: A value indicating what type of event this update represents.
member.remarks: Many events share the same response type. For example, and are both associated with a instance. You can use the value of to differentiate between these events when the type is not sufficient to do so.

member.summary: Indicates that there is no known reason associated with the streaming update.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Assistants.StreamingUpdateReason.ThreadCreated
member.summary: Indicates that an update was generated as part of a thread.created event.
member.remarks: This reason is typically only associated with calls to , as other run-related methods operate on a thread that has previously been created.

member.summary: Indicates that an update was generated as part of a thread.run.requires_action event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Audio.AudioTimestampGranularities
member.summary: Specifies the timestamp granularities to populate for a transcription.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Audio.AudioTimestampGranularities.Default
member.summary: The default value that, when equivalent to a request's flags, specifies no specific audio timestamp granularity and defers to the default timestamp behavior.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Audio.AudioTimestampGranularities.Word
member.summary: The value that, when present in the request's flags, specifies that audio information should include word-level timestamp information.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Audio.AudioTimestampGranularities.Segment
member.summary: The value that, when present in the request's flags, specifies that audio information should include segment-level timestamp information.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion
member.summary: A chat completion generated by the model.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.Object
member.summary: The object type, which is always `chat.completion`.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.CreatedAt
member.summary: The timestamp when the chat completion was created.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.FinishReason
member.summary:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be:
if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence
if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached
if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters
if the model called a tool
(obsolete) if the model called a function

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.ContentTokenLogProbabilities
member.summary: The message content tokens with log probability information.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.RefusalTokenLogProbabilities
member.summary: The message refusal tokens with log probability information.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.Role
member.summary: The role of the author of this message.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.Content
member.summary: The contents of the message.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.ToolCalls
member.summary: The tool calls generated by the model, such as function calls.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.Refusal
member.summary: The refusal message generated by the model.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletion.OutputAudio
member.summary: The audio response generated by the model.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions
member.summary: Request-level options for chat completion.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.Messages
member.summary: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far. [Example Python code](https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/how_to_format_inputs_to_chatgpt_models). Please note is the base class. According to the scenario, a derived class of the base class might need to be assigned here, or this property needs to be casted to one of the possible derived classes. The available derived classes include , , , and .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.Model
member.summary: ID of the model to use. See the model endpoint compatibility table for details on which models work with the Chat API.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.N
member.summary: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep `n` as `1` to minimize costs.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.Stream
member.summary: If set, partial message deltas will be sent, like in ChatGPT. Tokens will be sent as data-only [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#Event_stream_format) as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `data: [DONE]` message. [Example Python code](https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/how_to_stream_completions).

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.StreamOptions
member.summary: Gets or sets the stream options.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.IncludeLogProbabilities
member.summary: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the message content.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.TopLogProbabilityCount
member.summary: An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability. must be set to if this property is used.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.StopSequences
member.summary: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.LogitBiases
member.summary: Modifies the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion. It maps tokens (specified by their token ID in the tokenizer) to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. Mathematically, the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling. The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100 should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.ToolChoice
member.summary:
Specifies which tool is called by the model, if any. means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message. means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools. means the model must call one or more tools. The model can also be forced to call a specific tool by constructing a new instance of while passing the desired as a constructor parameter.
is the default behavior when no tools are present, while is the default if tools are present.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.AllowParallelToolCalls
member.summary: Whether to enable parallel function calling during tool use.
member.remarks: Assumed true if not otherwise specified.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.EndUserId
member.summary: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse. Learn more.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.MaxOutputTokenCount
member.summary: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, including visible output tokens and, on applicable models, reasoning tokens.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.Metadata
member.summary: Developer-defined tags and values used for filtering completions in the OpenAI Platform dashboard.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.StoredOutputEnabled
member.summary: Indicates whether to store the output of this chat completion request for use in model distillation or evals.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.ReasoningEffortLevel
member.summary: (o1 and newer reasoning models only) Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. Currently supported values are , , and . Reducing reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatCompletionOptions.ResponseModalities
member.summary: Specifies the content types that the model should generate in its responses.
member.remarks: Most models can generate text and the default ["text"] value, from , requests this. Some models like gpt-4o-audio-preview can also generate audio, and this can be requested by combining ["text","audio"] via the flags | .

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFinishReason
member.summary:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be:
Property REST Condition
stop The model encountered a natural stop point or provided stop sequence.
length The maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached.
content_filter Content was omitted due to a triggered content filter rule.
tool_calls
With no explicit tool_choice, the model called one or more tools that were defined in the request.
function_call (Deprecated) The model called a function that was defined in the request.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFinishReason.Stop
member.summary: Indicates that the model encountered a natural stop point or provided stop sequence.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFinishReason.Length
member.summary: Indicates that the model reached the maximum number of tokens allowed for the request.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFinishReason.ContentFilter
member.summary: Indicates that content was omitted due to a triggered content filter rule.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFinishReason.ToolCalls
member.summary: Indicates that the model called a function that was defined in the request.
member.remarks:
To resolve tool calls, append the message associated with the tool calls followed by matching instances of for each tool call, then perform another chat completion with the combined set of messages.
Note: is not provided as the finish_reason if the model calls a tool in response to an explicit tool_choice via . In that case, calling the specified tool is assumed and the expected reason is .

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFinishReason.FunctionCall
member.summary: Indicates that the model called a function that was defined in the request.
member.remarks: To resolve a function call, append the message associated with the function call followed by a with the appropriate name and arguments, then perform another chat completion with the combined set of messages.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunction
member.summary: Represents the definition of a function that the model may call, as supplied in a chat completion request.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunction.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of .
member.param: The name of the function.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunction.FunctionName
member.summary: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunction.FunctionDescription
member.summary: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunction.FunctionParameters
member.summary: Gets or sets the parameters.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunctionCall
member.summary: Represents an assistant call against a supplied that is needed by the model to continue the logical conversation.

remarks.para: Note that functions are deprecated in favor of tools and using instances with will enable the use of tool_calls via instead of this type.

The model makes a function_call in response to evaluation of supplied name> and description information in functions and is resolved by providing a new with matching functioning output on a subsequent chat completion request.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunctionCall.FunctionName
member.summary: The name of the function to call.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunctionCall.FunctionArguments
member.summary: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunctionChoice
member.summary: Represents a desired manner in which the model should use the functions defined in a chat completion request.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunctionChoice.#ctor(System.String,System.ClientModel.Primitives.JsonPatch@)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: The function name.
Keeps track of any properties unknown to the library.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunctionChoice.CreateNamedChoice(System.String)
member.summary: Creates an instance of that specifies the model must call a specific function, referred to via its name.
member.param: The name of the function the model must call.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Chat.ChatFunctionChoice.CreateAutoChoice
member.summary: Creates an instance of that specifies the model may freely pick between generating a message or calling a function.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole
member.summary: Represents the role of a chat completion message.

item.note: Instructions to the model that guide the behavior of future assistant messages. Replaces system in o1 and newer models.
Instructions to the model that guide the behavior of future assistant messages.
Input messages from the caller, typically paired with assistant messages in a conversation.
Output messages from the model with responses to the user or calls to tools or functions that are needed to continue the logical conversation.
Resolution information for a in an earlier that was made against a supplied .
Resolution information for a in an earlier that was made against a supplied . Note that functions are deprecated in favor of tool_calls.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole.System
member.summary: The system role, which provides instructions to the model that guide the behavior of future assistant messages

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole.User
member.summary: The user role that provides input from the caller as a prompt for model responses.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole.Assistant
member.summary: The assistant role that provides output from the model that either issues completions in response to user messages or calls provided tools or functions.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole.Tool
member.summary: The tool role that provides resolving information to prior tool_calls made by the model against supplied tools.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole.Function

summary.para: The function role that provides resolving information to a prior function_call made by the model against a definition supplied in functions.

member.remarks: functions are deprecated in favor of tools and supplying tools will result in tool_calls that must be resolved via the tool role rather than a function_call resolved by a function role message.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole.Developer
member.summary: The developer role, which provides instructions to the model that guide the behavior of future assistant messages
member.remarks: developer replaces system when using o1 and newer models.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatOutputAudio
member.summary: Represents the audio output generated by the model as part of a chat completion response.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatOutputAudioFormat
member.summary: Specifies the audio format the model should use when generating output audio as part of a chat completion response.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatOutputAudioReference
member.summary: Represents an ID-based reference to a past audio output as received from a prior chat completion response, as provided when creating an instance for use in a conversation history.
member.remarks: This value is obtained from the or properties for streaming and non-streaming responses, respectively. The constructor overload can also be used to automatically populate the appropriate properties from a instance.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatOutputAudioVoice
member.summary: Specifies the available voices that the model can use when generating output audio as part of a chat completion.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatOutputTokenUsageDetails
member.summary: A breakdown of the number of tokens used to generate the output as reported in .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatOutputTokenUsageDetails.ReasoningTokenCount
member.summary: The number of tokens consumed internally by the model for the purpose of reasoning.
member.remarks: Only applicable to models with reasoning capabilities, such as the OpenAI o1 series.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatOutputTokenUsageDetails.AudioTokenCount
member.summary: The number of audio tokens in the output.

The format that the model should output.

member.summary: Creates a new requesting plain text.

member.summary: Creates a new requesting adherence to the specified JSON schema, a.k.a. structured outputs.
member.param: The name of the response format.

param.name: jsonSchema
param.para: The schema of the response format, described as a JSON schema. Learn more in the structured outputs guide. and the JSON schema reference documentation.

param.name: jsonSchemaIsStrict
param.para: Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the response. If set to true, the model will follow the exact schema defined in .
Only a subset of the JSON schema specification is supported when this is set to true. Learn more in the structured outputs guide.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatResponseModalities
member.summary: Specifies the types of output content the model should generate for a chat completion request.
member.remarks:
Most models can generate text, which is the default . Some models like gpt-4o-audio-preview can also generate audio, which can be requested by combining
| .

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatResponseModalities.Default
member.summary: The value which specifies that the model should produce its default set of output content modalities.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatResponseModalities.Text
member.summary: The flag that, if included, specifies that the model should generate text content in its response.

member.name: F:OpenAI.Chat.ChatResponseModalities.Audio
member.summary: The flag that, if included, specifies that the model should generate audio content in its response.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenLogProbabilityDetails
member.summary: Represents a single token's log probability information, as requested via .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenLogProbabilityDetails.LogProbability
member.summary: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenLogProbabilityDetails.Utf8Bytes
member.summary: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be null if there is no bytes representation for the token.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenLogProbabilityDetails.TopLogProbabilities
member.summary: List of the most likely tokens and their log probability at this token position. In rare cases, there may be fewer than the number of requested top_logprobs returned, as supplied via .

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenTopLogProbabilityDetails
member.summary: Represents a single item of log probability information as requested via and .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenTopLogProbabilityDetails.LogProbability
member.summary: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenTopLogProbabilityDetails.Utf8Bytes
member.summary: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be null if there is no bytes representation for the token.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenUsage
member.summary: Represents computed token consumption statistics for a chat completion request.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenUsage.OutputTokenCount
member.summary: The combined number of output tokens in the generated completion, as consumed by the model.
member.remarks: When using a model that supports such as o1-mini, this value represents the sum of those reasoning tokens and conventional, displayed output tokens.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenUsage.InputTokenCount
member.summary: The number of tokens in the request message input, spanning all message content items.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenUsage.TotalTokenCount
member.summary: The total number of combined input (prompt) and output (completion) tokens used by a chat completion operation.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenUsage.OutputTokenDetails
member.summary: Additional information about the tokens represented by , including the count of consumed reasoning tokens by supported models.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.ChatTokenUsage.InputTokenDetails
member.summary: Additional information about the tokens represented by , including the count of audio tokens, if applicable to the model.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.DeveloperChatMessage
member.summary: Represents a chat message of the developer role as supplied to a chat completion request. A developer message is generally supplied as the first message to a chat completion request and guides the model's behavior across future assistant role response messages. These messages may help control behavior, style, tone, and restrictions for a model-based assistant. Developer messages replace system messages for o1 models and newer.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.InternalCreateChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice.FinishReason
member.summary: The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be `stop` if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence, `length` if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached, `content_filter` if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters, `tool_calls` if the model called a tool, or `function_call` (deprecated) if the model called a function.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate
member.summary: An incremental update corresponding to a streaming chat completion generated by the model.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.Object
member.summary: The object type, which is always `chat.completion.chunk`.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.CreatedAt
member.summary: The timestamp when the chat completion was created.
member.remarks: Each update has the same timestamp.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.Usage
member.summary: The token usage statistics for the entire chat completion.
member.remarks: Present only in the last streaming update.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.FinishReason
member.summary:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be:
if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence
if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached
if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters
if the model called a tool
(obsolete) if the model called a function

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.ContentTokenLogProbabilities
member.summary: The message content tokens with log probability information.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.RefusalTokenLogProbabilities
member.summary: The message refusal tokens with log probability information.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.Role
member.summary: The role of the author of this message.
member.remarks: Typically present in a single streaming update but applicable to the entire chat completion.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.ContentUpdate
member.summary: The contents of the message update.
member.remarks: Each streaming update contains only a small portion of tokens. To reconstitute the entire chat completion, all values across streaming updates must be combined.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.ToolCallUpdates
member.summary: The tool calls generated by the model, such as function calls.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.RefusalUpdate
member.summary: The refusal message generated by the model.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatCompletionUpdate.OutputAudioUpdate
member.summary: Incremental output audio generated by the model. Only expected when output audio has been requested via providing to and only available with supported models.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatFunctionCallUpdate.FunctionName
member.summary: The name of the function to call.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatFunctionCallUpdate.FunctionArgumentsUpdate
member.summary: Gets a function arguments fragment associated with this update.

remarks.para: Each update contains only a small number of tokens. When presenting or reconstituting a full, streamed arguments body, all values should be combined.

As is the case for non-streaming , the content provided for function arguments is not guaranteed to be well-formed JSON or to contain expected data. Callers should validate function arguments before using them.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatOutputAudioUpdate
member.summary: Represents an audio update in a streaming chat response.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatOutputAudioUpdate.TranscriptUpdate
member.summary: The next, incremental audio transcript part from the streaming response. payloads across all received instances should be concatenated to form the full response audio transcript.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatOutputAudioUpdate.AudioBytesUpdate
member.summary: The next, incremental response audio data chunk from the streaming response. payloads across all received instances should be concatenated to form the full response audio.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatToolCallUpdate
member.summary: A streaming update representing part of a tool call made by the model.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatToolCallUpdate.Kind
member.summary: The kind of tool.Currently, only is supported.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatToolCallUpdate.ToolCallId
member.summary: The unique identifier of the tool call being streamed, as used with e.g. or .

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatToolCallUpdate.FunctionName
member.summary: The name of the tool that the streaming tool call requests invocation of.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Chat.StreamingChatToolCallUpdate.FunctionArgumentsUpdate
member.summary: The update to the arguments that the model is calling the function with, which are generated by the model in JSON format. As part of a streaming response, the arguments are chunked and streamed across streaming updates, and must therefore be accumulated in order to reconstruct them. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

member.summary: Uploads a file that can be used across various operations.

The filename associated with the file stream. The filename's extension (for example: .json) will be used to validate the file format. The request may fail if the filename's extension and the actual file format do not match.

member.summary: Returned as a URI pointing to a temporary internet location from where the image can be downloaded. This URI is only valid for 60 minutes after the image is generated.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions
member.summary: Represents additional options available to control the behavior of an image edit operation.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions.Background
member.summary: Allows to set transparency for the background of the generated image(s).

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions.OutputCompressionFactor
member.summary: The compression level (0-100%) for the generated images.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions.OutputFileFormat
member.summary: The format in which the generated images are returned.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions.Quality
member.summary: The quality of the image that will be generated.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions.ResponseFormat
member.summary: The format in which the generated images are returned.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions.Size
member.summary: The size of the generated images.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageEditOptions.EndUserId
member.summary: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse. Learn more.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Images.ImageGenerationOptions
member.summary: Represents additional options available to control the behavior of an image generation operation.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageGenerationOptions.ModerationLevel
member.summary: Control the content-moderation level for the generated images.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageGenerationOptions.OutputCompressionFactor
member.summary: The compression level (0-100%) for the generated images.

member.name: P:OpenAI.Images.ImageGenerationOptions.OutputFileFormat

member.name: T:OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions
member.summary: The options to configure the client.

member.name: P:OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions.Endpoint
member.summary: The service endpoint that the client will send requests to. If not set, the default endpoint will be used.

member.name: P:OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions.OrganizationId
member.summary: The value to use for the OpenAI-Organization request header. Users who belong to multiple organizations can set this value to specify which organization is used for an API request. Usage from these API requests will count against the specified organization's quota. If not set, the header will be omitted, and the default organization will be billed. You can change your default organization in your user settings. Learn more.

member.name: P:OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions.ProjectId
member.summary: The value to use for the OpenAI-Project request header. Users who are accessing their projects through their legacy user API key can set this value to specify which project is used for an API request. Usage from these API requests will count as usage for the specified project. If not set, the header will be omitted, and the default project will be accessed.

member.name: P:OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions.UserAgentApplicationId
member.summary: An optional application ID to use as part of the request User-Agent header.

member.name: M:OpenAI.OpenAIClientOptions.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfigurationSection)
member.summary: Internal constructor for binding from a configuration section. Used by ClientSettings classes to bind nested "Options" section.

member.name: T:OpenAI.OpenAIHostBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for adding OpenAI clients with configuration and DI support following System.ClientModel pattern. See: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/System.ClientModel/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommand
member.summary: Represents a realtime client event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandConversationItemCreate
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.create client event. Add a new Item to the Conversation's context, including messages, function calls, and function call responses. This event can be used both to populate a "history" of the conversation and to add new items mid-stream, but has the current limitation that it cannot populate assistant audio messages. If successful, the server will respond with a conversation.item.created event, otherwise an error event will be sent.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandConversationItemDelete
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.delete client event. Send this event when you want to remove any item from the conversation history. The server will respond with a conversation.item.deleted event, unless the item does not exist in the conversation history, in which case the server will respond with an error.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandConversationItemRetrieve
member.summary:
Corresponds to the conversation.item.retrieve client event. Send this event when you want to retrieve the server's representation of a specific item in the conversation history. This is useful, for example, to inspect user audio after noise cancellation and VAD.
The server will respond with a conversation.item.retrieved event, unless the item does not exist in the conversation history, in which case the server will respond with an error.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandConversationItemTruncate
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.truncate client event. Send this event to truncate a previous assistant message's audio. The server will produce audio faster than realtime, so this event is useful when the user interrupts to truncate audio that has already been sent to the client but not yet played. This will synchronize the server's understanding of the audio with the client's playback. Truncating audio will delete the server-side text transcript to ensure there is not text in the context that hasn't been heard by the user. If successful, the server will respond with a conversation.item.truncated event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandInputAudioBufferAppend
member.summary:
Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.append client event. Send this event to append audio bytes to the input audio buffer. The audio buffer is temporary storage you can write to and later commit. A "commit" will create a new user message item in the conversation history from the buffer content and clear the buffer. Input audio transcription (if enabled) will be generated when the buffer is committed. If VAD is enabled the audio buffer is used to detect speech and the server will decide when to commit. When Server VAD is disabled, you must commit the audio buffer manually. Input audio noise reduction operates on writes to the audio buffer.
The client may choose how much audio to place in each event up to a maximum of 15 MiB, for example streaming smaller chunks from the client may allow the VAD to be more responsive. Unlike most other client events, the server will not send a confirmation response to this event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandInputAudioBufferClear
member.summary: Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.clear client event. Send this event to clear the audio bytes in the buffer. The server will respond with an input_audio_buffer.cleared event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandInputAudioBufferCommit
member.summary: Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.commit client event. Send this event to commit the user input audio buffer, which will create a new user message item in the conversation. This event will produce an error if the input audio buffer is empty. When in Server VAD mode, the client does not need to send this event, the server will commit the audio buffer automatically. Committing the input audio buffer will trigger input audio transcription (if enabled in session configuration), but it will not create a response from the model. The server will respond with an input_audio_buffer.committed event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandOutputAudioBufferClear
member.summary: Corresponds to the output_audio_buffer.clear client event. WebRTC/SIP Only: Emit to cut off the current audio response. This will trigger the server to stop generating audio and emit a output_audio_buffer.cleared event. This event should be preceded by a response.cancel client event to stop the generation of the current response.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandResponseCancel
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.cancel client event. Send this event to cancel an in-progress response. The server will respond with a response.done event with a status of response.status=cancelled. If there is no response to cancel, the server will respond with an error. It's safe to call response.cancel even if no response is in progress, an error will be returned the session will remain unaffected.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandResponseCreate
member.summary:
Corresponds to the response.create client event. This event instructs the server to create a Response, which means triggering model inference. When in Server VAD mode, the server will create Responses automatically.
A Response will include at least one Item, and may have two, in which case the second will be a function call. These Items will be appended to the conversation history by default.
The server will respond with a response.created event, events for Items and content created, and finally a response.done event to indicate the Response is complete.
The response.create event includes inference configuration like instructions and tools. If these are set, they will override the Session's configuration for this Response only.
Responses can be created out-of-band of the default Conversation, meaning that they can have arbitrary input, and it's possible to disable writing the output to the Conversation. Only one Response can write to the default Conversation at a time, but otherwise multiple Responses can be created in parallel. The metadata field is a good way to disambiguate multiple simultaneous Responses.
Clients can set conversation to none to create a Response that does not write to the default Conversation. Arbitrary input can be provided with the input field, which is an array accepting raw Items and references to existing Items.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeClientCommandSessionUpdate
member.summary: Corresponds to the session.update client event. Send this event to update the session's configuration. The client may send this event at any time to update any field except for voice and model. voice can be updated only if there have been no other audio outputs yet. When the server receives a session.update, it will respond with a session.updated event showing the full, effective configuration. Only the fields that are present in the session.update are updated. To clear a field like instructions, pass an empty string. To clear a field like tools, pass an empty array. To clear a field like turn_detection, pass null.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdate
member.summary: Represents a realtime server event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationCreated
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.created server event. Returned when a conversation is created. Emitted right after session creation.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemAdded
member.summary:
Corresponds to the conversation.item.added server event. Sent by the server when an Item is added to the default Conversation. This can happen in several cases:
When the client sends a conversation.item.create event.
When the input audio buffer is committed. In this case the item will be a user message containing the audio from the buffer.
When the model is generating a Response. In this case the conversation.item.added event will be sent when the model starts generating a specific Item, and thus it will not yet have any content (and status will be in_progress).
The event will include the full content of the Item (except when model is generating a Response) except for audio data, which can be retrieved separately with a conversation.item.retrieve event if necessary.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemCreated
member.summary:
Corresponds to the conversation.item.created server event. Returned when a conversation item is created. There are several scenarios that produce this event:
The server is generating a Response, which if successful will produce either one or two Items, which will be of type message (role assistant) or type function_call.
The input audio buffer has been committed, either by the client or the server (in server_vad mode). The server will take the content of the input audio buffer and add it to a new user message Item.
The client has sent a conversation.item.create event to add a new Item to the Conversation.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemDeleted
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.deleted server event. Returned when an item in the conversation is deleted by the client with a conversation.item.delete event. This event is used to synchronize the server's understanding of the conversation history with the client's view.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemDone
member.summary:
Corresponds to the conversation.item.done server event. Returned when a conversation item is finalized.
The event will include the full content of the Item except for audio data, which can be retrieved separately with a conversation.item.retrieve event if needed.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemInputAudioTranscriptionCompleted
member.summary:
Corresponds to the conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed server event. This event is the output of audio transcription for user audio written to the user audio buffer. Transcription begins when the input audio buffer is committed by the client or server (when VAD is enabled). Transcription runs asynchronously with Response creation, so this event may come before or after the Response events.
Realtime API models accept audio natively, and thus input transcription is a separate process run on a separate ASR (Automatic Speech Recognition) model. The transcript may diverge somewhat from the model's interpretation, and should be treated as a rough guide.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemInputAudioTranscriptionDelta
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.delta server event. Returned when the text value of an input audio transcription content part is updated with incremental transcription results.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemInputAudioTranscriptionFailed
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.failed server event. Returned when input audio transcription is configured, and a transcription request for a user message failed. These events are separate from other error events so that the client can identify the related Item.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemInputAudioTranscriptionSegment
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.segment server event. Returned when an input audio transcription segment is identified for an item.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemRetrieved
member.summary:
Corresponds to the conversation.item.retrieved server event. Returned when a conversation item is retrieved with conversation.item.retrieve.
This is provided as a way to fetch the server's representation of an item, for example to get access to the post-processed audio data after noise cancellation and VAD. It includes the full content of the Item, including audio data.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateConversationItemTruncated
member.summary: Corresponds to the conversation.item.truncated server event. Returned when an earlier assistant audio message item is truncated by the client with a conversation.item.truncate event. This event is used to synchronize the server's understanding of the audio with the client's playback. This action will truncate the audio and remove the server-side text transcript to ensure there is no text in the context that hasn't been heard by the user.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateError
member.summary: Corresponds to the error server event. Returned when an error occurs, which could be a client problem or a server problem. Most errors are recoverable and the session will stay open, we recommend that implementers monitor and log error messages by default.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateInputAudioBufferCleared
member.summary: Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.cleared server event. Returned when the input audio buffer is cleared by the client with a input_audio_buffer.clear event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateInputAudioBufferCommitted
member.summary: Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.committed server event. Returned when an input audio buffer is committed, either by the client or automatically in server VAD mode. The item_id property is the ID of the user message item that will be created, thus a conversation.item.created event will also be sent to the client.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateInputAudioBufferDtmfEventReceived
member.summary: Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.dtmf_event_received server event. SIP Only: Returned when an DTMF event is received. A DTMF event is a message that represents a telephone keypad press (0–9, *, #, A–D). The event property is the keypad that the user press. The received_at is the UTC Unix Timestamp that the server received the event.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateInputAudioBufferSpeechStarted
member.summary: Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.speech_started server event. Sent by the server when in server_vad mode to indicate that speech has been detected in the audio buffer. This can happen any time audio is added to the buffer (unless speech is already detected). The client may want to use this event to interrupt audio playback or provide visual feedback to the user. The client should expect to receive a input_audio_buffer.speech_stopped event when speech stops. The item_id property is the ID of the user message item that will be created when speech stops and will also be included in the input_audio_buffer.speech_stopped event (unless the client manually commits the audio buffer during VAD activation).

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateInputAudioBufferSpeechStopped
member.summary: Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.speech_stopped server event. Returned in server_vad mode when the server detects the end of speech in the audio buffer. The server will also send an conversation.item.created event with the user message item that is created from the audio buffer.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateInputAudioBufferTimeoutTriggered
member.summary:
Corresponds to the input_audio_buffer.timeout_triggered server event. Returned when the Server VAD timeout is triggered for the input audio buffer. This is configured with idle_timeout_ms in the turn_detection settings of the session, and it indicates that there hasn't been any speech detected for the configured duration.
The audio_start_ms and audio_end_ms fields indicate the segment of audio after the last model response up to the triggering time, as an offset from the beginning of audio written to the input audio buffer. This means it demarcates the segment of audio that was silent and the difference between the start and end values will roughly match the configured timeout.
The empty audio will be committed to the conversation as an input_audio item (there will be a input_audio_buffer.committed event) and a model response will be generated. There may be speech that didn't trigger VAD but is still detected by the model, so the model may respond with something relevant to the conversation or a prompt to continue speaking.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateMcpListToolsCompleted
member.summary: Corresponds to the mcp_list_tools.completed server event. Returned when listing MCP tools has completed for an item.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateMcpListToolsFailed
member.summary: Corresponds to the mcp_list_tools.failed server event. Returned when listing MCP tools has failed for an item.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateMcpListToolsInProgress
member.summary: Corresponds to the mcp_list_tools.in_progress server event. Returned when listing MCP tools is in progress for an item.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateOutputAudioBufferCleared
member.summary: Corresponds to the output_audio_buffer.cleared server event. WebRTC/SIP Only: Emitted when the output audio buffer is cleared. This happens either in VAD mode when the user has interrupted (input_audio_buffer.speech_started), or when the client has emitted the output_audio_buffer.clear event to manually cut off the current audio response.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateOutputAudioBufferStarted
member.summary: Corresponds to the output_audio_buffer.started server event. WebRTC/SIP Only: Emitted when the server begins streaming audio to the client. This event is emitted after an audio content part has been added (response.content_part.added) to the response.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateOutputAudioBufferStopped
member.summary: Corresponds to the output_audio_buffer.stopped server event. WebRTC/SIP Only: Emitted when the output audio buffer has been completely drained on the server, and no more audio is forthcoming. This event is emitted after the full response data has been sent to the client (response.done).

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateRateLimitsUpdated
member.summary: Corresponds to the rate_limits.updated server event. Emitted at the beginning of a Response to indicate the updated rate limits. When a Response is created some tokens will be "reserved" for the output tokens, the rate limits shown here reflect that reservation, which is then adjusted accordingly once the Response is completed.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseContentPartAdded
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.content_part.added server event. Returned when a new content part is added to an assistant message item during response generation.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseContentPartDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.content_part.done server event. Returned when a content part is done streaming in an assistant message item. Also emitted when a Response is interrupted, incomplete, or cancelled.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseCreated
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.created server event. Returned when a new Response is created. The first event of response creation, where the response is in an initial state of in_progress.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.done server event. Returned when a Response is done streaming. Always emitted, no matter the final state. The Response object included in the response.done event will include all output Items in the Response but will omit the raw audio data. Clients should check the status field of the Response to determine if it was successful (completed) or if there was another outcome: cancelled, failed, or incomplete. A response will contain all output items that were generated during the response, excluding any audio content.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseFunctionCallArgumentsDelta
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.function_call_arguments.delta server event. Returned when the model-generated function call arguments are updated.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseFunctionCallArgumentsDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.function_call_arguments.done server event. Returned when the model-generated function call arguments are done streaming. Also emitted when a Response is interrupted, incomplete, or cancelled.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseMcpCallArgumentsDelta
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.mcp_call_arguments.delta server event. Returned when MCP tool call arguments are updated during response generation.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseMcpCallArgumentsDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.mcp_call_arguments.done server event. Returned when MCP tool call arguments are finalized during response generation.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseMcpCallCompleted
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.mcp_call.completed server event. Returned when an MCP tool call has completed successfully.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseMcpCallFailed
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.mcp_call.failed server event. Returned when an MCP tool call has failed.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseMcpCallInProgress
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.mcp_call.in_progress server event. Returned when an MCP tool call has started and is in progress.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputAudioDelta
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_audio.delta server event. Returned when the model-generated audio is updated.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputAudioDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_audio.done server event. Returned when the model-generated audio is done. Also emitted when a Response is interrupted, incomplete, or cancelled.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputAudioTranscriptDelta
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_audio_transcript.delta server event. Returned when the model-generated transcription of audio output is updated.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputAudioTranscriptDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_audio_transcript.done server event. Returned when the model-generated transcription of audio output is done streaming. Also emitted when a Response is interrupted, incomplete, or cancelled.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputItemAdded
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_item.added server event. Returned when a new Item is created during Response generation.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputItemDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_item.done server event. Returned when an Item is done streaming. Also emitted when a Response is interrupted, incomplete, or cancelled.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputTextDelta
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_text.delta server event. Returned when the text value of an "output_text" content part is updated.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateResponseOutputTextDone
member.summary: Corresponds to the response.output_text.done server event. Returned when the text value of an "output_text" content part is done streaming. Also emitted when a Response is interrupted, incomplete, or cancelled.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateSessionCreated
member.summary: Corresponds to the session.created server event. Returned when a Session is created. Emitted automatically when a new connection is established as the first server event. This event will contain the default Session configuration.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Realtime.RealtimeServerUpdateSessionUpdated
member.summary: Corresponds to the session.updated server event. Returned when a session is updated with a session.update event, unless there is an error.

member.summary: Represents a encapsulating the data from a WebSocket response message.
member.remarks: WebSocket messages can be split across multiple network receive operations and a single may thus ingest and present data across several such operations.

member.summary: The value to use for the OpenAI-Organization request header. Users who belong to multiple organizations can set this value to specify which organization is used for an API request. Usage from these API requests will count against the specified organization's quota. If not set, the header will be omitted, and the default organization will be billed. You can change your default organization in your user settings. Learn more.

Usage from these API requests will count as usage for the specified project. If not set, the header will be omitted, and the default project will be accessed.

member.name: T:OpenAI.AsyncSseUpdateCollection`1

member.name: M:OpenAI.DataEncodingHelpers.ParseDataUriRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^data:(?<type>.+?);base64,(?<data>.+)$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match the string "data:".
○ "type" capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily at least once.
○ Match the string ";base64,".
○ "data" capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' greedily at least once.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.
